title: Communication Studies

jokes.text: But remember, cupid's face is magic marker on nylon, So love is not only blind But also dizzy and a little belligerent.
jokes.setup: Valentine's Day PA announcement about the Greendale Human Being as cupid
jokes.punchline: Love being dizzy and belligerent because cupid's face is drawn on with marker
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that uses the physical reality of the Human Being mascot to create a joke about love's nature

dialogue.speaker: PA Announcer
dialogue.line: Love being dizzy and belligerent because cupid's face is drawn on with marker.

jokes.text: What's the blonde's name, bitter, butter--beetlejuice? Oh, britta.
jokes.setup: Character trying to remember Britta's name
jokes.punchline: Goes through similar-sounding words including 'Beetlejuice'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation gag with pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta? Brit... Brita? Like the water filter? Beetlejuice? Beatrice? Brenda?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: It's Britta.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Right, that's what I said.

jokes.text: She says valentine's day ritualizes a connection Between affection and candy So girls can learn the ropes of prostitution.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Britta doesn't like Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Her over-the-top feminist critique connecting candy to prostitution
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific humor that reveals Britta's tendency toward extreme political interpretations

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Valentine's Day is just a capitalist construct designed to commodify love and reduce women to objects of consumption. It's basically legalized prostitution with chocolate.

jokes.text: Britta, drunkenly: - Jeff winger. I am calling you. [chuckling] You're probably--whatever. [in a raspy voice] so wassup?
jokes.setup: Britta leaving a drunk voicemail
jokes.punchline: The awkward, rambling nature and casual 'wassup'
jokes.explanation: Establishes the drunk dial plot point with character-appropriate awkwardness

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Hey, it's me. So I'm at this thing, and I'm thinking about you, and I just... I wanted to call and say... wassup? No, that's not— I'm not good at this sober, and I'm definitely not good at this drunk. Which, by the way, I'm not drunk. I'm just... I've had some drinks. Some drinks that I'm responsible about. Anyway, what I'm trying to say is... I miss you? Or I'm angry at you? One of those. Definitely one of those. Call me back. Or don't. Either way is fine. It's not fine. It's really not fine.

jokes.text: Oh, now it has arrows. That's safe.
jokes.setup: The Human Being mascot now carrying arrows as cupid
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation about the safety hazard
jokes.explanation: Efficient visual gag commentary with good economy of language

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: A mascot with arrows? That's a serious safety hazard.

jokes.text: I guess when he said, [in a gravely voice] I'm gonna git you, [cheerfully] uh, he meant chocolates.
jokes.setup: Scary-sounding threat from cafeteria cook
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection from threatening to romantic, with good voice contrast

dialogue.speaker: Cafeteria Cook
dialogue.line: I'm going to break your legs.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Cafeteria Cook
dialogue.line: I'm going to break your legs... so you can't run away from my love.

jokes.text: Meryl streep has two oscars because of her baking.
jokes.setup: Discussion about actresses sending muffins to get roles
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Meryl Streep's success is due to baking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed delivery - deadpan absurdism that sounds plausible in his voice

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Meryl Streep didn't get where she is today by sending muffins. She baked them fresh on set.

jokes.text: Oh, that's sarcasm, but I forgot to inflect. This sounds way more like sarcasm. Inflection is so interesting.
jokes.setup: Abed analyzing his own sarcasm
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on inflection while demonstrating it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor that's perfectly in character for Abed's analytical nature

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The thing about sarcasm is that it's all in the delivery. Like, if I say 'Oh great, another paintball war,' with the right inflection, everyone knows I'm being sarcastic.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: But if I say it like this—'Oh great, another paintball war'—it just sounds sincere, which defeats the entire purpose of the sarcasm, thus making the meta-commentary on my own use of sarcasm ironic because I'm now being sincere about something I was just being sarcastic about.

jokes.text: Barnes, troy? Barney miller?
jokes.setup: Troy desperately looking for Valentine's gifts
jokes.punchline: Suggests 'Barney Miller' as variation of his name
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's desperation and naivety through pop culture reference confusion

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, what if I got her something with my name in it? Like a personalized gift?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I could get her a 'Barney Miller' DVD set. You know, as a variation of Troy Barnes?

jokes.text: It destroys the true meaning of valentine's day: The birth of saint valentines.
jokes.setup: Shirley criticizing materialism of Valentine's Day
jokes.punchline: Gets the religious significance completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate religious misunderstanding that reveals Shirley's earnest but confused nature

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Valentine's Day is just a commercialized holiday designed to make us buy things we don't need. It's so materialistic and un-Christian.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know, it's supposed to be about Saint Valentine, who died for our credit card debt.

jokes.text: Can I get you something? Water, smelling salts, An alibi for cobain's suicide?
jokes.setup: Offering help to hungover Britta
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal hangover remedies to conspiracy theory alibi
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke with dark humor that fits Jeff's snarky character

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You need water, aspirin, and a big greasy breakfast.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Actually, you need to say you were at the library all night. Building a false digital footprint — deleted emails, fabricated library records.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We should probably get you a burner phone just in case someone asks follow-up questions about your whereabouts.

jokes.text: No, but you can help yourself to a shorter forehead, A non-keebler nose, and shutting up.
jokes.setup: Britta responding to Jeff's jabs while hungover
jokes.punchline: Specific insults culminating in 'shutting up'
jokes.explanation: Creative insults with good specificity, though 'Keebler nose' is particularly inventive

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You look terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I feel terrible. I drank too much last night.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You always drink too much. Your liver must hate you.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: My liver and I have an understanding.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Yeah, mutual destruction. You're slurring your words, your makeup looks like a crime scene, and I'm pretty sure you're still drunk.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Okay, you know what? I'm just going to shut up now.

jokes.text: Mm, that stings. I mean, not the words. The clouds of bourbon vapor forming them.
jokes.setup: Appearing hurt by Britta's insults
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually her alcohol breath that stings
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with poetic language about bourbon vapor

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Ouch, that really hurt.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Good.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, I mean your breath. Is that whiskey or paint thinner?

jokes.text: That's, uh, booty call implication.
jokes.setup: Explaining what B.C.I. means
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation of sexual subtext
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed clinical analysis of social situations

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: B.C.I. stands for 'Butts, Coitus, and Innuendo.'

jokes.text: Having spent the year denying her attraction to me / Just to be alternative, britta called me. / At 3:00 a.M. Just to ask, / [imitates britta] 'what's up?'
jokes.setup: Jeff's interpretation of Britta's motivations
jokes.punchline: His arrogant reduction of her complex personality to attraction denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff narcissism - reducing everything to being about him and his attractiveness.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: She's just denying her attraction to me. It's a classic defense mechanism. Deep down, Britta knows she wants me, but her insecurities won't let her admit it. So she lashes out, acts superior, pretends to be above it all. It's really quite transparent when you understand psychology like I do.

jokes.text: You've shifted the balance, like in a sitcom When one character sees another one naked.
jokes.setup: Analyzing the drunk dial situation
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to a sitcom trope while being in a sitcom
jokes.explanation: Classic Community meta-humor - Abed analyzing sitcom tropes while in one

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You know what this is? This is the drunk dial episode. It's a classic sitcom trope where someone calls another character while intoxicated and says things they normally wouldn't say.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The thing is, we're living in a sitcom, so we're going to have to do this eventually. It's inevitable.

jokes.text: Is that really a sitcom staple? - No, I have no idea what I'm talking about. I'm abed, I never watch tv.
jokes.setup: Jeff questioning Abed's sitcom knowledge
jokes.punchline: Abed sarcastically claiming he never watches TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic humor since Abed is the show's TV expert making a sarcastic denial

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You're telling me you don't know this classic sitcom trope?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Oh no, I never watch TV. I just have an encyclopedic knowledge of every show ever made through osmosis and pure instinct.

jokes.text: Yeah, I said, 'we do not study countries in here.'
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining how he met his fake girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Confusing geology with geography
jokes.explanation: Shows Pierce's age and confusion in character-appropriate way

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I met her at a geology convention.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I thought it was a geology convention. Turns out it was geography. I don't know the difference.

jokes.text: Beep, beep, beep, beep, beep. My bullcrap meter's going crazy.
jokes.setup: Chang detecting the fake Valentine's gifts
jokes.punchline: Pretending to have a bullcrap detector making beeping sounds
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy bit that establishes Chang's theatrical cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I have a bullcrap detector. Beep beep beep beep beep!

jokes.text: And yours is signed, 'love, troy'?
jokes.setup: Chang examining Troy's fake love note
jokes.punchline: Troy literally signed his own name to his fake girlfriend's note
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Troy's naive mistake

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You signed your own name? To a fake girlfriend's love note?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I panicked!

jokes.text: Cutting the gristle off his steak As ruthlessly as he cut off troy and pierce's dignity. Ew! Now he's eating the gristle.
jokes.setup: Metaphor about Chang cutting steak like dignity
jokes.punchline: The gross detail that he then eats the gristle
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor with disgusting escalation

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Chang is cutting that steak like dignity — carefully, methodically, with respect.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: And then he eats the gristle.

jokes.text: Hey. Moustache. Get me a juice box. Now!
jokes.setup: Annie deciding to take revenge on Chang
jokes.punchline: Demanding cafeteria worker get her juice box like a bratty child
jokes.explanation: Shows Annie's righteous anger in characteristically childish way

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I want my juice box. Now. And I want it in the little plastic pouch with the straw attached.

dialogue.speaker: Cafeteria Worker
dialogue.line: We don't have juice boxes.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Then make one happen. I'm not leaving this line until you do.

jokes.text: I got so drunk that I got into a fight With animatronic ben franklin.
jokes.setup: Jeff telling story to make Britta feel better about drunk behavior
jokes.punchline: Fighting with a Disney World robot
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable Jeff story with specific amusing detail

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I once got so drunk at Disney World that I fought with a robot.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, but I'm trying to make you feel better about yourself.

jokes.text: Beep, beep, beep. What is going on here? My doody meter's going crazy.
jokes.setup: Actor copying Chang's bullcrap meter bit
jokes.punchline: Says 'doody meter' instead of bullcrap meter
jokes.explanation: Weak callback that relies on childish word substitution

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: This is my bullcrap meter. When someone's talking bullcrap, the needle goes up.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: So when I say 'doody meter'... wait, no.

jokes.text: One papa john's commercial, And he thinks he's christian bale.
jokes.setup: Actor being difficult during Abed's film
jokes.punchline: Comparing minor commercial work to Christian Bale's ego
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about actor ego with good pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Look, I get it. You did a commercial for dish soap once. That doesn't make you Christian Bale.

dialogue.speaker: Actor
dialogue.line: Hey, that commercial was very well-received!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, Christian Bale would never be this difficult on set. He'd just yell at the crew and move on.

jokes.text: Tony even saw angela naked on the opening credits Of who's the boss?
jokes.setup: Abed explaining sitcom dynamics
jokes.punchline: Reference to Who's the Boss opening credits
jokes.explanation: Typical Abed TV analysis with obscure but accurate reference

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: See, in a traditional sitcom, you need a clear power dynamic. Someone's the boss, someone's not. It creates tension, conflict, comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Like 'Who's the Boss?' The whole show is right there in the title. Tony Danza's the housekeeper, but he's also kind of the boss of the household dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's the fundamental sitcom equation. Hierarchy equals humor.

jokes.text: I could never get past the opening credits.
jokes.setup: Discussing Who's the Boss
jokes.punchline: Admitting he never actually watched the show
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan follow-up that subverts his expertise

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I've seen every episode of Who's the Boss.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No. I've never actually watched it.

jokes.text: All right. I'll show britta my penis.
jokes.setup: Trying to solve the sitcom balance problem
jokes.punchline: Taking Abed's advice too literally
jokes.explanation: Blunt escalation that shows Jeff's literal interpretation

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm going to take Abed's advice and find better balance in my life.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's great. What are you going to do?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Well, I'm going to stand on one leg for exactly eight hours a day.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not what I meant by balance.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Too late. I've already started a spreadsheet.

jokes.text: [slurred speech] I can totally do it. I'm fine. I can drive. I can totally drive right now. Give me my keys.
jokes.setup: Jeff demonstrating his drunk acting
jokes.punchline: Perfect stereotypical drunk person denial
jokes.explanation: Standard drunk acting trope but well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not drunk. Why would you say that? I'm completely fine. I can walk in a straight line, see? *stumbles* That's just the floor being uneven. And my words are coming out perfectly normal, thank you very much.

jokes.text: He's a young 'the asian guy' from lost.
jokes.setup: Commenting on the difficult actor
jokes.punchline: Reference to unnamed Asian actor from Lost
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works for character but isn't exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: He's like that unnamed Asian character from Lost. He'll show up in two episodes, be really important, then disappear and nobody will ever mention him again.

jokes.text: They could be put off by her vacuous mannequin face. And her jodie foster severity.
jokes.setup: Abed analyzing why people don't like Britta
jokes.punchline: Specific and harsh but accurate descriptions
jokes.explanation: Abed's brutal honesty with creative and specific descriptors

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: People don't like Britta because she's the worst. She's like if a well-meaning person and a wrecking ball had a baby, and that baby was raised by a TED talk.

jokes.text: She has more fights about stuff that doesn't matter Than a youtube comment section.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing Britta's argumentative nature
jokes.punchline: Comparison to YouTube comments
jokes.explanation: Sharp modern comparison that perfectly captures Britta's nature

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Britta argues about everything. She's like a YouTube comments section that gained consciousness and learned to wear a cardigan.

jokes.text: I can't feel things With you studying me like a beige praying mantis.
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about Abed's intense observation
jokes.punchline: Specific and unusual comparison to beige praying mantis
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and specific visual metaphor with unexpected color detail

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, do you ever stop observing people? It's like being watched by a beige praying mantis.

jokes.text: Scorsese drank with de niro.
jokes.setup: Justifying why he'll drink with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Comparing their situation to legendary filmmaker duo
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-appropriate film reference

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's like we're Scorsese and De Niro. Except you're the one who needs the drink.

jokes.text: Looks like the law firm of seacrest and slumdog's Taking the day off.
jokes.setup: Chang seeing Jeff and Abed hungover
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference law firm name
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference but feels forced

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You two look like you got hit by a bus.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We feel like it.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: You should sue. I know a great law firm: Lionel Hutz and Associates.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's from The Simpsons.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: So? He's still a lawyer.

jokes.text: They're telling me I can name my price? Oh, my god. At princeton? Come on!
jokes.setup: Chang reading fake Princeton offer
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top excitement at obviously fake letter
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Chang's desperation and ego

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Princeton? PRINCETON?! Oh my god, oh my god, oh my god! I got into Princeton! Me! Chang! Princeton wants ME! This is the most beautiful day of my entire life! I'm going to be a Princeton man! An Ivy Leaguer! Oh, the prestige! The tradition! The... wait, what does this say? 'Congratulations on being selected for our exciting new online diploma program'? ONLINE?! That's even BETTER! I don't even have to leave! This is perfect!

jokes.text: Tonight, you are my bitches.
jokes.setup: Chang punishing Troy and Pierce
jokes.punchline: Crude declaration of dominance
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate power play from Chang

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: I am the law in this school!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: That's... not how any of this works.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Silence! I will dropkick the next person who speaks!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You can't even reach our faces from down there.

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Then I'll find a stepladder and dropkick you anyway!

jokes.text: You were dancing like that girl In that movie... The kids in detention? - Breakfast club.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to remember what happened while drunk
jokes.punchline: Vague description of iconic movie dance scene
jokes.explanation: Mild hangover humor with pop culture reference

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What happened last night? I was completely wasted.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You did an iconic movie dance scene.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I don't know, some vague description that could apply to like five different movies.

jokes.text: Molly ringworm.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to remember Molly Ringwald's name
jokes.punchline: Says 'Ringworm' instead of 'Ringwald'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay showing Jeff's continued hangover confusion

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Ringworm? Wait, no... Ringwald. Molly Ringwald.

jokes.text: I still can't believe I'm a size 14. I know that 12's a little tight, But I could have pulled it off.
jokes.setup: Pierce trying on women's clothes for Chang's punishment
jokes.punchline: His concern about dress size rather than the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce vanity even in humiliating situation

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Wait, what size is this dress? I'm not trying on anything that makes me look fat.

jokes.text: Women's sizes run slimmer. I'm sure you're a 12 everywhere that it counts.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to comfort Pierce about dress sizes
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual comfort about his body
jokes.explanation: Annie's innocent attempt at comfort becomes unintentionally suggestive

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, don't be upset. There's nothing wrong with your body.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You're a big, strong man. Women like that.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Your size is actually really impressive.

jokes.text: You look about ready to marry courtney love.
jokes.setup: Seeing Pierce in drag
jokes.punchline: Specific pop culture comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comparison with grunge reference

jokes.text: That's the reverse of my zinger from before.
jokes.setup: Shirley commenting on her own joke
jokes.punchline: Self-aware analysis of her callback structure
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about joke structure, which is unusual for Shirley

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know, I just realized I made a callback to my own joke from three episodes ago. That's the kind of self-aware humor that really separates the Shirley Bennetts from the... well, everyone else.

jokes.text: I was trying to call britain to order you toffees.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining mistaken phone call to Michelle
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse about calling Britain for toffees
jokes.explanation: Creatively absurd excuse that sounds like something Jeff would say

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Look, I can explain. I was trying to call Britain to order some toffees. I got confused about the time zones, thought I'd reach them during business hours. Instead I got your mom's cell phone. Apparently your area code is very similar to London's. Also, I may have been drinking.

jokes.text: It sure seems like you and britta are friends The way my mom's pool cleaner was my uncle.
jokes.setup: Michelle suspicious about Jeff and Britta's relationship
jokes.punchline: Comparison to mother's affair with pool cleaner
jokes.explanation: Dark and specific comparison that reveals family dysfunction casually

jokes.text: First of all, chubby hubby? I mean, could you pick a scarier flavor?
jokes.setup: Michelle mentioning ice cream flavor
jokes.punchline: Jeff's fear of commitment-themed ice cream name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff commitment-phobia expressed through ice cream flavor

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not getting that one. It sounds like it's going to ask me about my feelings.

jokes.text: Well, nice fix, Tweedle Dum and even Tweedler Dum
jokes.setup: Annie angry at girls for making situation worse
jokes.punchline: Creative variation on Tweedledee and Tweedledum insult
jokes.explanation: Creative insult variation though not particularly sharp

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You know what? You two are like Tweedledee and Tweedledum, except instead of being identical twins, you're identical idiots.

jokes.text: Just look away, baby. Just look away.
jokes.setup: Troy and Pierce walking to the dance in drag
jokes.punchline: Troy's dramatic protection of Annie and Shirley's innocence
jokes.explanation: Troy's sweet attempt to maintain dignity in absurd situation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Annie, Shirley, you two need to avert your eyes. This is adult content.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We're going to shield you from the harsh realities of the world by doing the most inappropriate thing possible in front of you.

jokes.text: girlfriend, girlfriend. I'm sorry. Abed just made a turtle face.
jokes.setup: Jeff's drunk voicemail being played
jokes.punchline: Random observation about Abed's turtle face
jokes.explanation: Random drunk observation that feels authentically intoxicated

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Hey, it's me. I'm calling from the bar. I just wanted to say... Abed's got a turtle face. Like, have you noticed that? A turtle face. I'm not even drunk, I'm just... observing. Anyway, call me back.

jokes.text: That was the first 20 seconds of a 40-minute message
jokes.setup: Playing Jeff's embarrassing drunk message
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was much longer than expected
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing the full extent of Jeff's drunk rambling

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Hey Britta, it's me. I just wanted to say that you're really great and I think you're really smart and funny and—

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Okay, that's sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: —and I love the way you laugh, and I love the way you look at me, and sometimes when you're not looking I look at you, and I think about—

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: How long is this message?

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: There's more.

jokes.text: You said he hated fro-yo. Just drive.
jokes.setup: Troy and Pierce's awkward post-dance situation
jokes.punchline: Pierce's insistence on frozen yogurt despite Troy's discomfort
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment that suggests romantic undertones

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Pierce, we don't need to go to frozen yogurt right now.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Are you kidding? After all that dancing, we earned it!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I'm really uncomfortable and—

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Uncomfortable? Troy, frozen yogurt fixes everything. It's basically medicine.

jokes.text: Slut!
jokes.setup: Pierce and Troy walking together after the dance
jokes.punchline: Student calling out what appears to be a romantic couple
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment but lacks sophistication

dialogue.speaker: Random student
dialogue.line: Hey, get a room!

tmdb_overview: As Valentine's Day finds Jeff struggling with his feelings for Britta and Professor Slater, Pierce and Troy are wrongly blamed for a prank.
insight: Early season stumble: 52 jokes in 22 minutes but sub-71 score reveals execution gaps.
